title: Diego's Big Adventure
synopsis: Diego gets lost in the forest but with the help of friendly animal friends, he finds his way home and learns that being brave means asking for help when you need it.

pages.text: Diego was playing in his backyard when he saw a beautiful blue butterfly. "Come back!" he called as it flew into the tall trees. Diego followed the butterfly deeper and deeper into the forest, laughing and running.

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with short dark hair wearing a red t-shirt and blue shorts chasing a bright blue butterfly into a lush green forest. Diego looks happy and excited with a big smile on his face. The forest has tall trees with textured bark and dappled sunlight filtering through the leaves.
illustration.alt: Diego chasing a butterfly into the forest

pages.text: When Diego stopped running, the butterfly was gone. He looked around and felt scared. The trees were so tall and everything looked the same. "Mama? Papa?" he called, but no one answered. Diego felt sad and worried.

illustration.description: Diego standing alone in the middle of the forest looking small and worried. His eyes are wide and his mouth is turned down in a sad expression. The trees tower above him and shadows create a slightly darker mood. His hands are pressed together in front of him nervously.
illustration.alt: Diego looking lost and scared in the forest

pages.text: Just then, Diego heard a friendly voice. "Hello there!" said Zara the zebra, stepping out from behind a tree. Next to her was Oliver the owl perched on a branch, and Maya the monkey swinging down from above. "You look lost," said Maya kindly.

illustration.description: Three friendly animals approaching Diego: a zebra with black and white stripes and kind eyes, a brown owl with large round eyes wearing tiny glasses, and a playful monkey with dark fur. All the animals have warm, welcoming expressions. The forest setting is brighter and more colorful around them.
illustration.alt: Friendly animals meeting Diego in the forest

pages.text: "I am lost," Diego said, feeling a little less scared. "I followed a butterfly and now I can't find my way home." Zara smiled gently. "Don't worry, we can help you! Being brave means asking for help when you need it."

illustration.description: Diego talking to the three animals, his expression changing from worried to hopeful. Zara the zebra is nuzzling Diego gently with her nose. Oliver the owl looks wise and reassuring, while Maya the monkey gives an encouraging thumbs up. The scene feels warm and supportive.
illustration.alt: The animals comforting and reassuring Diego

pages.text: Oliver flew high above the trees to look for Diego's house. "I can see it!" he hooted down. Maya showed Diego which way to go, and Zara walked beside him to keep him company. Diego felt brave and happy with his new friends helping him.

illustration.description: Oliver the owl flying high above the forest canopy with his wings spread wide, pointing with one wing toward a small house visible in the distance. Below, Diego walks confidently with Zara beside him and Maya pointing the way. Everyone looks determined and happy.
illustration.alt: The animals helping Diego find his way home

pages.text: Soon Diego could see his backyard! His mama and papa were there looking worried. "Diego!" they called happily when they saw him. Diego ran to hug them, feeling so happy and safe. He turned to thank his animal friends, but they had quietly disappeared back into the forest. Diego smiled, knowing he had been very brave today.

illustration.description: Diego running toward his parents with his arms outstretched, his face beaming with joy. His mama, a Latina woman with long dark hair in a yellow dress, and his papa, a Latino man with a mustache wearing a green shirt, are kneeling with their arms open wide. In the background, the three animal friends can be seen waving goodbye from the edge of the forest.
illustration.alt: Diego reuniting with his parents while animal friends wave goodbye

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: What did Diego follow into the forest?
questions.explanation: Diego followed a beautiful blue butterfly into the forest, which is how he got lost.

questions.question: How did Diego feel when he realized he was lost?
questions.explanation: Diego felt scared and sad when he couldn't find his way home and no one answered when he called.

questions.question: Which animal flew high to look for Diego's house?
questions.explanation: Oliver the owl flew high above the trees to look for Diego's house and spotted it from above.

questions.question: What did Zara say about being brave?
choices: Being brave means asking for help when you need it
questions.explanation: Zara told Diego that being brave means asking for help when you need it, which is an important lesson.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete Diego's adventure!
pages.text: Diego followed a blue _____ into the forest. When he got lost, he felt _____ and sad. Three friendly _____ helped him find his way home. Diego learned that being _____ means asking for help.
